issue_title: config:push with .env doesn't handle export or double quotes
issue_body:
If you have environment variables in your .env file that are surrounded by double quotes or start with an export they aren't handled correctly when pushed to deis. For example:

export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"

ends up as

export REDIS_URL: "redis://localhost:6379"

Where "export REDIS_URL" is the key and the value is still surrounded by double quotes which can then cause things to fail when the config is referenced.

issue_title: Healthcheck failure on deploy not reported.
issue_body:
If I deploy a new version of my application and that application fails it's health check, the `git push` output does not inform me that this has happened.  It just rolls back silently and prints the usual "deployed to Deis" message:

```
-----> Launching...
       done, app:v73 deployed to Deis

       http://url

       To learn more, use `deis help` or visit http://deis.io

To ssh://myserver:2222/repo.git
 * [new branch]      master -> master
```

The only way to tell a deploy has failed is to notice that the version number in the message has not incremented.  This might have been behind the issue I was experiencing in #4841

issue_title: Integration tests should be table-driven
issue_body:
Deis' integration tests boil down to "run this command line and inspect the return code and stdout/stderr." This maps nicely to a common go idiom: table-driven tests. Refactoring the tests this way would make them much more approachable.

I've already begun working on this and intend to finish it for the next Deis release.

issue_title: Best way to support multiple versions of the same app. (eg: DEV, QA, PROD)
issue_body:
Have a Deis cluster, and want to configure/release apps for different purposes. I believe I can use .env to set parameters that the app uses (eg: which database it talks to and which versions of other services)... but is there a good way to switch between them?  Is there a way to have three versions of app foo running?

eg: git push deis prod for prod, git push deis qa for qa (and have them pick up the appropriate environment?)

These are go apps so they live in the go path source directory (otherwise I could just have three copies of the git repo with different ENVs)

What's the best practice for this?

Yes, I know prod should probably be its own cluster and it will eventually but we will at least still need DEV and QA.

issue_body:
### What was wrong?

The `encode_abi` and `decode_abi` functions (found [here](https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/abi.py#L55-L74) and [here](
https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/abi.py#L136-L160)) do not really serve a useful purpose any more.  For example, the following invocations are equivalent:
```python
In [1]: from eth_abi import *

In [2]: encode_single('(int,int)', (1, 2))

In [3]: encode_abi(('int', 'int'), (1, 2))
Out[3]: b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x02'
```

### How can it be fixed?

Perhaps we should remove `encode_abi` and `decode_abi` and rename `encode_single` and `decode_single` to `encode` and `decode` respectively?

issue_title: New contract method execution option: generating the transaction dictionary
issue_body:
* Version: 4

### What was wrong?

There is no convenient way to prepare a contract transaction for offline signing.

Specifically, the prepared transaction should work smoothly with [`w3.eth.account.signTransaction()`](http://web3py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/web3.eth.account.html#web3.eth.account.Account.signTransaction).


### How can it be fixed?

#### New contract method preparation API

Add a new way to prepare a contract function transaction. It would look something like:

```
# new API
transaction_dict = myContract.functions.myMethod().asdict()

# existing APIs
signed = w3.eth.account.signTransaction(transaction_dict, private_key)
w3.eth.sendRawTransaction(signed.rawTransaction)
```

`asdict` should accept a dictionary that defines transaction fields, just like call/transact/etc, and return an `AttributeDict`.

#### Fields included

Besides the natural fields, like `to` and `data`, this must also generate other fields like:

- nonce
- chainId
- gas
- gasPrice

If they are not supplied as arguments to `asdict()`, then they should be inferred before returning. `signTransaction` will not attempt to infer these values, so they must be explicitly added.

#### API Naming

`asdict()` is okay, but isn't necessarily the obvious winner.

Some alternatives:

- `myContract.functions.myMethod().todict()`
- `myContract.functions.myMethod().tofields()`
- `myContract.functions.myMethod().prepare()`
- `myContract.functions.myMethod().generate()`

#### Crossover issue

Depending on whether #419 is already implemented, and how it's implemented, this might require a bit of extra work to expose `asdict` in both ways.

#### TODOs

- [ ] choose naming
- [ ] implement new API
- [ ] document in `docs/`
- [ ] reference this method in signTransaction docs `docs/web3.eth.account.rst`
- [ ] Support for #419 - if implementation available

issue_title: Quantifier checks not always looking for RSTR role.
issue_body:
Pred, ElementaryPredication, and Node components all have an `is_quantifier()` method, but only in ElementaryPredication does it look for the RSTR role on its arguments (because only EPs have arguments as part of the data structure).

Where the information is available (e.g. in converting from MRS to DMRS), the RSTR role should only be used in determining if something is a quantifier.

We should possibly also deprecate `is_quantifier()` on Node and Pred, and instead use something like `xmrs.nodeids(quantifier=True)` in order to get this information. If someone wants to use the generally-but-not-necessarily-true assumption that a Node or Pred with `q` as the pos value is a quantifier, they can look directly at `pred.pos`.

issue_title: Relocatable conda environments
issue_body: I had a good conversation with @airnandez from CNRS IN2P3 French supercomputing center.  They are big Anaconda fans, but are blocked in some areas in their ability to use it because of how software gets provisioned onto their cluster.  They need to create the conda environments in one file system with a path prefix `/a/b` then move to `/c/d` on the "deployed" system.  There is some good discussion about this issue in #361 and @airnandez and I came up with some observations about `--clone` and `conda create` that make me feel like it should be feasible to package up an environment in a way that it can be relocated via some intermediate mechanics.

issue_title: "conda run" command removed?
issue_body:
Previously we were able to use `conda run -n envname -- toolname arg1 arg2` to run a specific tool in a specific environment without activating it first. Conveniently, this worked even in a different active environment and allowed the temporary use of py27-dependent code within an otherwise py3 environment.

However in conda 4.1.2, I get the error, `Error: Could not locate 'conda-run'`. I have been unable to find documentation for `conda run`, or any release notes indicating it has been removed or even git log entries indicating it was removed. It was there at least in 3.7.0, haven't dug though individual commits yet to see exactly where it was lost.

Currently my workaround is to provide the full path to the program within its environment dir (which requires knowing the user's conda installation path), or to downgrade conda. Is there any replacement for conda-run that I'm missing?

issue_title: Optional requirements?
issue_body:
Sounds like an oxymoron.  I started thinking about this just now when I did `conda install matplotlib`.  I'm planning to use matplotlib exclusively in the IPython notebook, so I won't be using the Qt backend for matplotlib.  However, Qt is installed because it is a dependency of matplotlib.  Since Qt is so large, downloading the package took the bulk of the total install time.

Is there a way to mark that some dependencies will only be needed by some users?  I'm not sure I can think of a good way to handle this sort of situation.  Maybe a separate package called `matplotlib-Qtbackend` would be sufficient, but then some users will be quite surprised to find that installing matplotlib and Qt is not sufficient to get them a Qt backend.  What if the matplotlib and Qt packages both knew to look for the other when they were installed, and if both are present, to install the backend package?

I'm just brainstorming.

issue_title: conda remove doesn't complain if only some of the packages don't exist
issue_body:
If I do `conda remove foo bar` and neither of them are in the current environment then I get an error.  However if I do `conda remove foo bar` and `foo` is present but `bar` is not then `foo` gets removed but there is no error or warning that `bar` wasn't there and was not removed.  I discovered this because I misunderstood the behavior of the `--features` flag, so it also applies to removing features.

I would suggest that a warning is required as a minimum, and perhaps it should be an error to be "symmetric" with a `conda install foo bar` where `foo` exists to be installed but `bar` does not (which just reports an error, and does not install anything).

issue_title: Rstudio does not run from Anaconda
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x ] bug report ## Current Behavior When I try to run RStudio from Anaconda Navigator or Anaconda Prompt, RStudio seems lo launch but then a "Download file" window pops up (showing the Rstudio icon at the top) and I am forced to select a file. Previously I did not encounter this issue and RStudio did work from Anaconda. I do not understand what RStudio wants me to select. If I press Cancel, RStudio launching is aborted. I do not see an error, I just get stuck on the "Download File" window. (if I select for example the Rstudio.exe file, RStudio is still aborted). I do not understand this behavior. issue_title: Broken compatibility with GLIBC in CentOS6 after "conda update --all"
issue_body:
After updating all packages in Conda in CentOS6 I found that the minimum version required of glibc library is now 2.7 when it used to be <2.4! 

If I try any conda command I get the infamous error:
/home/cpl/miniconda2/bin/python: /lib64/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.7' not found (required by /home/cpl/miniconda2/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0)

On my machine:
>ldconfig --version
ldd (GNU libc) 2.5

I checked libpython2.7.so.1.0:
>objdump -T ~/miniconda2/lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0 | grep GLIBC_2.7
0000000000000000      DF *UND*	0000000000000000  GLIBC_2.7   __fread_chk

So it seems that symbol is breaking the compatibility. Any idea if that is a bug or an intended upgrade? Where is that symbol coming from and why is it using 2.7 libc interface?

issue_title: confusing error message when boto is missing
issue_body:
If one tries to install from an s3 channel (e.g., `conda install foo -c s3://bar/baz/conda`) while in an environment, this message shows up:  `Error: boto is required for S3 channels. Please install it with: conda install boto`.  Installing `boto` in the environment doesn't actually solve the problem, however, because `boto` has to be installed into the main set of packages (e.g., `source deactivate; conda install boto`).

Perhaps a simple edit to the error message would address this.  (e.g., `Error: boto is required for S3 channels. Please install it with: conda install boto.  Make sure to run "source deactivate" if you are in an environment.`)

issue_title: activate breaks on unbound vars in some bash shells
issue_body:
The deactivate script [attempts to access $CONDA_PATH_BACKUP](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/402ace6f77775f2d7965128a0f7f42243142f32c/shell/deactivate#L55) even when it isn't bound (for example, [when `activate` sources `deactivate`](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/402ace6f77775f2d7965128a0f7f42243142f32c/shell/activate#L59)). This makes `deactivate` exit with an error message when sourced from a bash shell with [the `nounset` option](http://tldp.org/LDP/abs/html/options.html) set. Eventually `activate` breaks when the `errexit` option is also set:

```
$ /bin/bash -c 'source activate testenv; echo $CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV'
testenv
$ /bin/bash -eu -c 'source activate testenv; echo $CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV'
/home/jenkins-ci/miniconda3/bin/deactivate: line 55: CONDA_PATH_BACKUP: unbound variable
$ 
```

When sourced in a non-interactive session, `activate` does the same thing all by itself (even with `changeps1: False`):

```
$ /bin/bash -eu -c 'CONDA_PATH_BACKUP=""; source activate testenv; echo $CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV'
/home/jenkins-ci/miniconda3/bin/activate: line 65: PS1: unbound variable
$
```

It's not crazy to activate a conda env in such a shell. `nounset` and `errexit` are helpful and fairly common when debugging, and they help avoid silent failures in production. 

Some options are:
1. Ask users to activate conda environments in shells without `nounset` and `errexit` options set,
2. Bind these variables at the beginning of each script if they're unbound, or
3. Fix these two tests to [check for unbound variables](http://stackoverflow.com/a/13864829).

Option (3) seems least disruptive to me. If there are no objections, I'll open a PR soon.

issue_title: YAML Lint fails silently
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
If there is a syntax error in the YAML, it should throw an error rather than exiting silently.

### Actual Behavior
In some situations, curator runs and exits without any output if supplied with a configuration in which there is a YAML syntax error.

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem

1. Install `elasticsearch-curator-5.5.4-1.x86_64.rpm` on an offline RHEL 7.4 (Maipo) machine
2. Run with a yaml file with a syntax error, e.g.:

```
client:
  hosts: ["10.10.10.10"]
  port: 9200
  use_ssl: True
  certificate:'/etc/elasticsearch/ca.crt'
```
(Note the minor syntax error here: there should be a space after the `:` on the `certificate` line)

3. When curator is run (saving the config in `~/.curator/curator.yml`, it will return 

```
curator_cli show_indices --loglevel DEBUG
or
curator --config ~/.curator/curator.yml --dry-run ./shrink.yml
```
It simply exits without any output to log or console.

Note that this seems to be related to the installation method - on a similar `pip` install the issue does not manifest.

issue_title: AzureStack: images aliases is not applicable for azurestack
issue_body:
### Description
CLI has a set of aliases for the images referred from the following location
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/master/arm-compute/quickstart-templates/aliases.json

These aliases makes the assumption that these images are available in all the clouds and matches the 
definition like below
          "UbuntuLTS":{
            "publisher":"Canonical",
            "offer":"UbuntuServer",
            "sku":"16.04-LTS",
            "version":"latest"
          }

In azure stack , the alias does not make much sense as the admin may not have uploaded the same image
in azure stack the Ubuntu server sku could be 14.04.3 instead of 16.04-LTS

luckily , the image parameter can be specified with the whole urn like below and this is what being used in azurestack 
Canonical:UbuntuServer:14.04.3-LTS:1.0.0

All the public documentation is referring to the use of aliases and the aliases most likely wont work against azurestack unless the admin chooses to upload image matching the azure definitions

issue_title: Lots of CancelledError when requests are interupted
issue_body:
## Long story short

aiohttp barfs out hundreds of `concurrent.futures._base.CancelledError` when requests are interrupted.

## Expected behaviour

Not sure, I guess we can't just ignore `CancelledError` in requests but it would be good if there was some way to stop loads of errors being printed.

This is annoying in the terminal but could cause a major backlog if sentry etc. were connected and processing exceptions.

You can catch and ignore this error with middleware:

```python
async def cancelled_error_middleware(app, handler):
    async def _handler(request):
        try:
            return await handler(request)
        except CancelledError:
            raise HTTPBadRequest()
    return _handler
```

But I'm not sure how safe this is?

## Actual behaviour

```
[2017-01-30 12:22:19 +0000] [30723] [ERROR] Error handling request
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/web_server.py", line 61, in handle_request
    resp = yield from self._handler(request)
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/web.py", line 249, in _handle
    resp = yield from handler(request)
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/frameworks/Python/aiohttp/app/views.py", line 142, in updates
    .values(randomnumber=randint(1, 10000))
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiopg/utils.py", line 72, in __await__
    resp = yield from self._coro
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiopg/sa/connection.py", line 110, in _execute
    yield from cursor.execute(str(compiled), post_processed_params[0])
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiopg/cursor.py", line 113, in execute
    yield from self._conn._poll(waiter, timeout)
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiopg/connection.py", line 240, in _poll
    raise exc
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiopg/connection.py", line 237, in _poll
    yield from asyncio.wait_for(self._waiter, timeout, loop=self._loop)
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.py", line 379, in wait_for
    yield from waiter
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 358, in __iter__
    yield self  # This tells Task to wait for completion.
  File "uvloop/future.pyx", line 434, in uvloop.loop.BaseTask._fast_wakeup (uvloop/loop.c:114015)
  File "/home/vagrant/FrameworkBenchmarks/installs/py3/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 266, in result
    raise CancelledError
concurrent.futures._base.CancelledError
```

Coming through thousands of times as I run `wrk` load tests. I suspect the same would occur with ajax interrupted requests or even browsers stopping a request if a new one is requested.

## Steps to reproduce

```
wrk -t12 -c100 -d10s http://localhost:8000/whatever
```

While running your aiohttp application with gunicorn.

## Your environment

Ubuntu.

issue_title: Disable DNS cache
issue_body:
If servers are hosted on Amazon DNS caches first answered IP.
In its turn Amazon uses round-robin to iterate over all IPs registered in load balancer.

Therefore aiohttp with cache enabled sends all payload to only one server from registered group.

The solution might don't use DNS cache for threaded DNS resolver and respect TTL returned by server for `aiodns` based implementation.

To implement the feature the moving DNS caching into resolver makes sense.

issue_title: missing nbextension commands
issue_body:
we have enable (set true)/disable (set false), but no mechanism to *delete* extension config. Example use case: You have previously enabled an extension at the user level and want to switch to env-level or system-wide, there is no mechanism to remove the user-level config other than editing JSON directly.

There's also no mechanism to see *installed* extensions (those in share/nbextensions), only *enabled* extensions, so it's hard to ask the question "do you have multiple copies of this library installed?"

issue_title: Possible with different font settings for editing code and markdown cells in the Jupyter Notebook?
Enhancement: Add line break after The Jupyter Notebook is running at:
issue_body:
[I 13:47:40.278 NotebookApp] The Jupyter Notebook is running at: http://localhost:8888/?token=b5d714ae8bd101fd251053ddc55add86fafb8e0c1ddd690f
is displayed in the console on starting jupyter notebook and if the browser has not been set or cannot be found the user needs to copy and paste this into a browser.

The trouble is that the standard console or command window width is 80 characters while the message is 142 which, on many systems, results in the text wrapping which is not convenient for copy and paste. 

Splitting the message over 2 lines should enable the entire text that needs to be copied to appear on a single line.  If some other tools use the output format adding a backslash at the end of the first line should satisfy most if not all of them.

issue_title: Argument splitting should not be done on space
issue_body:
There are some places where toil-vg passes string representations of argument lists for other programs. These really shouldn't be just split on space; they need to be parsed in a way that respects quotations and internal spaces (for example, if someone is trying to specify a contig name that contains a space).

Instead of string splitting, `shlex.split()` should be used. 

Instances of string joining should be eliminated; there's no need for the user argument merging functions to return strings that are immediately split again.

issue_title: Dict comprehension prevents library usage on Centos6.9 (python 2.6)
issue_body:
Hi,

There is a dict comprehension used here : 
File "/usr/lib/python2.6/site-packages/f5/bigip/resource.py", line 724
no_meta_dict = {k: v for k, v in iteritems(self.__dict__)

This prevents the library from being used on Centos6.9 with Python 2.6.

This is a big issue for us as since Ansible 2.4 update, all BigIP modules uses f5-sdk library and we can not use them anymore on our Centos6.9 servers.

I have opened this issue on Ansible side : https://github.com/ansible/ansible/issues/31341 which was closed as this is an issue on this side.

Is Python 2.6 still supported? If yes, dict comprehension should not be used

Thanks,

Ludo

issue_title: add websocket urls endpoint to asm policy
bundling general fixtures with the SDK made test too tightly coupled to function
Clustering is needed for creating a device service group cluster of multiple BigIPs
issue_body:
We need this feature for several customers. Given multiple BigIP objects, the cluster will perform the following operations:

* Make the BigIPs trusted peers of one another in preparation for clustering
* Create a device service group (or device group in the SDK) to sync failover objects
* Ensure each BigIP device is in the state that we expect it to be in after the previous steps

issue_title: Product class nullable?
issue_body:
A fellow developer saw the [code](https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/apps/catalogue/abstract_models.py#L267) for the product - product class relationship and assumed product class can be set to `None`. AFAIK, that is not the case and we rely on it.
I realize why we need to set it to be nullable for the deletion, but that leaves us with products in an awkward state. Is a simple docstring enough?

issue_title: The Postal Code Validator for Brunei Darussalam is Malformed
issue_body:
In oscar.apps.address.abstract_models.AbstractAddress:

```
POSTCODES_REGEX = {
    ...
    'BN': r'^[A-Z}{2}[0-9]]{4}$',
    ...
}
```

This should instead read

```
POSTCODES_REGEX = {
    ...
    'BN': r'^[A-Z]{2}[0-9]{4}$',
    ...
}
```

issue_title: NET Core 2.0 no longer support redhat 7.2 and OSX 10.11
issue_body:
Hi,

From the [netcore 2.0 support OS doc](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/roadmap.md#net-core-20---supported-os-versions), looks like Redhat only support 7.3+, OSX only support 10.12+.
While in netcore 1.0, Redhat support 7.2 and OSX support 10.11.

I would like to know what's the motivation for no longer support those OS version in netcore 2.0, this makes adopt net core 2.0 a little bit hard for us, since i don't want to break customer who still in those OS version.

issue_title: ObfuscationAttribute and ObfuscateAssemblyAttribute have wrong definitions in System.Runtime assembly
issue_body:
# System.Runtime assembly provides wrong definitions of obfuscation attributes

_System.Reflection.ObfuscationAttribute_ and _System.Reflection.ObfuscateAssemblyAttribute_ are defined without _AttributeUsage_ declarations. This breaks the expected attribute usage semantics.

# General

.NET Core version: 2.0
OS version: Windows 10 1702 15063.674
Visual Studio version: 15.4.1

Related assembly:
**System.Runtime,** Version=4.2.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a
located at _C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\NuGetFallbackFolder\microsoft.netcore.app\2.0.0\ref\netcoreapp2.0\System.Runtime.dll_

Please note that provided sample compiles and works as expected in .NET and .NET Standard 2.0 platforms.

## Steps to reproduce

Try to compile the following .NET Core 2.0 console app:

```c#
using System;
using System.Reflection;

[assembly: Obfuscation(Feature = "Feat 1")]
[assembly: Obfuscation(Feature = "Feat 2")]

namespace MyApp
{
    class Program
    {
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("Hello World!");
        }
    }
}

```

issue_title: Perl code in Apache config causes letsencrypt-auto to fail
issue_body:
The widely used request-tracker4 package on Debian Jessie ships the file /etc/request-tracker4/apache2-modperl2.conf which contains the following at the very end:

> \<Perl\>
>   use Plack::Handler::Apache2;
>   Plack::Handler::Apache2->preload("/usr/share/request-tracker4/libexec/rt-server");
> \</Perl\>

The second line within the <Perl> tag causes the following error when running letsencrypt-auto with the apache plugin:
> The apache plugin is not working; there may be problems with your existing configuration.
> The error was: PluginError(('There has been an error in parsing the file (%s): %s', u'/etc/request-tracker4/apache2-modperl2.conf', u'Syntax error'),)

Commenting out the line gets the letsencrypt client to work, but in my Opinion, it shouldn't fail because the config file is perfectly valid.

If you need further information, please let me know.

Thanks,
Martin

issue_title: Don't use the default UA when verifying challenges
issue_body: While related to #858, I feel like this is a slightly different issue. We have had reports of people running into problems with the User-Agent when using the `manual` plugin. `manual` uses `acme` to validate that the challenge has been set up correctly before responding to the server. This is done using the `requests` library whose default UA as of writing this e-mail is `python-requests/2.8.1`. Some webservers are rejecting requests with this UA, causing self validation to fail, and `letsencrypt` to exit.

issue_title: Surviving "Commit failed: Broker: Specified group generation id is not valid" on Broker restart
issue_body:
* Version 0.11.4 of both confluent-kafka-python and librdkafka.
* Ubuntu Trusty.
* Kafka 0.11.0.0 (CP 3.3.0), 3 brokers, replication=3, min.insync=2

```
consumer_config = {
    'enable.auto.commit': False,
    'bootstrap.servers': bootstrap_server,
    'group.id': group_id,
    'default.topic.config': {
        'auto.offset.reset': 'error',
    }
}
```

My manually committing consumer is unable to survive a broker stopping. Maybe this is expected, but I'm not sure how people typically handle this use case:

* I poll until I have a desirable batch size to write to an external DB.
* I write the batch to the external DB.
* I call `consumer.commit(asynchronous=False)` to ensure the matching offsets are committed to the broker.

(Note that I write a batch and commit to Kafka at minimum every ~10 seconds, and the flush to external DB happens in under ~2 seconds. I don't think I'm running up against any timeouts. My brokers run pretty much the default configuration.)

The issue is I'd like for my consumer to survive a broker restart, but I get the following when I stop one of my 3 brokers and I was in the middle of a DB write (which means a commit happens seconds later):

```
2018-05-07 17:37:27,135 140218522564480 Flushing 13588 items: forced:False size:False time:True
2018-05-07 17:37:28,574 140218522564480 Worker flush took 1438ms, committing to Kafka
2018-05-07 17:37:39,399 140218522564480 Flushing 10606 items: forced:False size:False time:True
%3|1525714660.065|FAIL|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:192.168.208.112:9092/3]: 192.168.208.112:9092/3: Receive failed: Disconnected
%3|1525714660.065|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:192.168.208.112:9092/3]: 192.168.208.112:9092/3: Receive failed: Disconnected
%3|1525714660.166|FAIL|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:192.168.208.112:9092/3]: 192.168.208.112:9092/3: Connect to ipv4#192.168.208.112:9092 failed: Connection refused
%3|1525714660.166|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:192.168.208.112:9092/3]: 192.168.208.112:9092/3: Connect to ipv4#192.168.208.112:9092 failed: Connection refused
2018-05-07 17:37:40,420 140218522564480 Worker flush took 1019ms, committing to Kafka
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "consumer.py", line 197, in _flush
    self.consumer.commit(asynchronous=False)
KafkaException: KafkaError{code=ILLEGAL_GENERATION,val=22,str="Commit failed: Broker: Specified group generation id is not valid"}
```

What's more confusing, is that after that occurs it seems like the offsets in the broker are corrupted or removed, because when I restart my consumer I get the following:

```
2018-05-07 17:43:44,120 139943834724224 New partitions assigned: [TopicPartition{topic=snuba,partition=0,offset=-1001,error=None}, TopicPartition{topic=snuba,partition=1,offset=-1001,error=None}, TopicPartition{topic=snuba,partition=2,offset=-1001,error=None}]
2018-05-07 17:43:44,122 139943834724224 KafkaError{code=_NO_OFFSET,val=-168,str="no previously committed offset available"}
2018-05-07 17:43:44,122 139943834724224 KafkaError{code=_NO_OFFSET,val=-168,str="no previously committed offset available"}
2018-05-07 17:43:44,122 139943834724224 KafkaError{code=_NO_OFFSET,val=-168,str="no previously committed offset available"}
```

And then, of course, nothing happens. So I have to reset to earliest or latest (or manually find out the correct offsets).

(Note that without a broker stop I can run multiple consumers, start and stop them regularly, and everything including offset storage is working just fine.)

So I have two questions:
* What's the proper way to survive `Commit failed: Broker: Specified group generation id is not valid`? In some of my research it seems like my only option is to continue to `poll()` and wait? But if so then how do I know when is safe to commit? And by polling wouldn't I be popping new messages off the queue and thus moving the offsets further than what I just wrote to my DB? Is there a "right way" to do what I'm trying to do that I'm missing?
* On a `Commit failed: Broker: Specified group generation id is not valid` I would have expected nothing to be committed, but it seems to break all of my stored offsets? Is that expected?

issue_title: Incompatibility with other mixins
issue_body:
* drf-sideloading version: `0.1.5`
* Django version: `__all__`
* Python version:  `__all__`
* Operating System: ` __all__`

### Description

`SideloadableRelationsMixin` is calling `self.get_serializer_class()` while initializing in `__init__`

https://github.com/namespace-ee/drf-sideloading/blob/960f66542b3903076db02d387a56cd9de66db71d/drf_sideloading/mixins.py#L13

This causes incompatibility with other mixins which might also override same `get_serializer_class` function.

For example if there is OtherMixin like this one :

```python
class OtherMixin(object):

    def get_serializer_class(self):
        if self.action in [u'user_permissions']:
           # ... some code .. # 
        return super(OtherMixin, self).get_serializer_class()
```

usecase in ViewSet


```python
class SomeViewSet(SideloadableRelationsMixin, OtherMixin, , viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    ...

```



This will cause `AttributeError` on `self.action` because serialiser has not yet finished initialising 

> AttributeError: 'SomeViewSet' object has no attribute 'action' 


### What I should be done

remove `self.get_serializer_class()` call from `SideloadableRelationsMixin` `__init__`

issue_title: KeyError: 'transifex_project_name' when updating .tx/config
issue_body:
When I was updating the txconfig using 

```python
$ sphinx-intl create-txconfig
$ sphinx-intl update-txconfig-resources --transifex-project-name python-35-tw
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
  File "/Users/liang/.virtualenvs/pydoc/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sphinx_intl/commands.py", line 536, in update_txconfig_resources
    args = [arg % locals() for arg in args_tmpl]
  File "/Users/liang/.virtualenvs/pydoc/lib/python3.5/site-packages/sphinx_intl/commands.py", line 536, in <listcomp>
    args = [arg % locals() for arg in args_tmpl]
KeyError: 'transifex_project_name'
```

It failed with KeyError, which the argument should be passed to the function.
A quick survey showed it might be related with using `locals()` inside list comprehension,

```python
# sphinx_intl/commands.py:536
args = [arg % locals() for arg in args_tmpl]
```

If I change to 

```python
d = locals()
args = [arg % d for arg in args_tmpl]
```

then the code works.


A demo shows this different behavior in Python 2 and 3.

```python
def print_dict(d):
    print(d)

def main():
    a, b = 3, 4
    print_dict(locals())
    [print_dict(locals()) for i in range(2)]

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

# python 2.7
# {'a': 3, 'b': 4}
# {'a': 3, 'i': 0, 'b': 4}
# {'a': 3, 'i': 1, 'b': 4}

# python 3.4 / 3.5
# {'a': 3, 'b': 4}
# {'.0': <range_iterator object at 0x1029851e0>, 'i': 0}
# {'.0': <range_iterator object at 0x1029851e0>, 'i': 1}
```

My Python packages versions:

```
Sphinx==1.3.3
sphinx-intl==0.9.7
transifex-client==0.11
```

issue_title: "Type" validation?
issue_body: Do you plan to validate item `type`s as part of your schema definition? The JSON API spec requires raising a 409 when there is a `type` mismatch. A nice way to handle this might be with a custom exception type (that subclasses `ValueError` instead of `ValidationError`) that applications can catch and re-raise as a 409 (it'd be a bit tricker to figure this out from a generic `ValidationError`).

issue_title: Enabling proofs per solver instance
issue_body:
We use Z3 via the API with a single context in which we put all our formulas. We create multiple solver instances, in some of which we need proofs for interpolation. Thus we have to set the option `PROOF` globally with `setParamValue`.

I assume that solving is more efficient if proof generation is turned off. Thus it would be good for our use case if we could enable it individually for each solver instance, and not just globally for the whole context.

issue_body:
The following code snippet is used to find the index some select statement in an expression is reading from. 

```
def find_select_index(expr):
    if is_app_of(expr, Z3_OP_SELECT):
        return expr.children()[1]
    else:
        for x in expr.children():
            i = find_select_index(x)

            if (i != -1):

issue_title: BaseMoneyValidator fails to validate falsy ints and Decimals
issue_body:
In [the validator code](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/master/djmoney/models/validators.py#L32), any subclass of `BaseMoneyValidator` will short-circuit the validation if the validator was initialised with a falsy integer or or decimal.

Attach a debugger at this code:

    if not limit_value:  # 0 or Decimal('0')
        return

Steps to reproduce:

1. Create a `MoneyField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default_currency='USD')` (copied from example) but with an additional validator, like so:

    ```
    MoneyField(
        max_digits=10, decimal_places=2,
        default_currency='USD',
        validators=[
            MinMoneyValidator(0),
        ]
    )
    ```

1. Make a migration and migrate.
1. Run the server and save any object with that new field with the Money equivalent of `(-1, 'USD')`. Observe that the validator does not raise a `ValidationError`.

This affects the common use case of validating against negative amounts of money.

issue_title: Sentinel 3 SLSTR reading the solar/viewing angles fails
issue_body:
When using the `nc_slstr` reader the solar and viewing angles aren't loaded properly. 

Given a loaded SLSTR scene, the available datasets are:
```python
scn.available_dataset_names()

['S1', 'S2', 'S3', 'S4', 'S5', 'S6', 'S7', 'S8', 'S9', 'latitude', 'longitude', 
'satellite_azimuth_angle', 'satellite_zenith_angle', 'solar_azimuth_angle', 
'solar_zenith_angle']
```` 
But indexing the scene with `scn['solar_azimuth_angle']` results in a `KeyError`.


After digging around a bit I noticed three issues. 

First, there is a "mapper dictionary" for the datatset names  in the `NCSLSTRAngles` class which looks like:

```python
    datasets = {'satellite_azimuth_angle': 'satellite_azimuth_t' + view,
                'satellite_zenith_angle': 'satellite_zenith_t' + view,
                'solar_azimuth_angle': 'solar_azimuth_t' + view,
                'solar_zenith_angle': 'solar_zenith_t' + view}
```

Based on the SLSTR products I have this should be `sat_`, instead of `satellite_`:

```python
    datasets = {'satellite_azimuth_angle': 'sat_azimuth_t' + view,
                'satellite_zenith_angle': 'sat_zenith_t' + view,
                'solar_azimuth_angle': 'solar_azimuth_t' + view,
                'solar_zenith_angle': 'solar_zenith_t' + view}
```

Secondly, the `_FillValue` attribute doesn't exist for the `geometry_tn.nc`, and also not for the `cartesian_*.nc` products. The missing values have are filled with `NaN`, so this could be fixed with:

`variable.attrs.get('_FillValue', np.nan)`

That would still use the `_FillValue` attribute if it's present in the attributes of the dataset, and otherwise assume `np.nan`.


Lastly, the `NCSLSTRAngles` class returns what looks like a deprecated `Dataset` class, but SatPy seems to expect an `xarray` dataset. By peaking at the OLCI reader, I changed the old code:

```python
        proj = Dataset(values,
                       copy=False,
                       units=units,
                       platform_name=self.platform_name,
                       standard_name=variable.attrs['standard_name'],
                       sensor=self.sensor)
        return proj
```
to:
```python
        proj = xr.DataArray(da.from_array(values, chunks=(CHUNK_SIZE, CHUNK_SIZE)),
                            dims=['y', 'x'])

        proj.attrs['platform_name'] = self.platform_name
        proj.attrs['sensor'] = self.sensor

        proj.attrs.update(key.to_dict())

        return proj
```

I made a branch on my fork which has these changes implemented, but i'm not sure if i fixed everything conform the SatPy standards:
https://github.com/RutgerK/satpy/tree/fix_slstr

Additionally that branch also uses `os.path.sep` in the `yaml reader` which is necessary on Windows,  otherwise SatPy won't find any files:

These are some of the files I tried this on:
```
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180628T102931_20180628T103231_20180629T155259_0179_033_008_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180629T100320_20180629T100620_20180630T152011_0179_033_022_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180630T093709_20180630T094009_20180701T151640_0179_033_036_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180702T102546_20180702T102846_20180703T150049_0179_033_065_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
```

**Environment Info:**
 - OS: Window 10 64bit
 - SatPy Version: 0.9.0 (py35)
 - PyResample Version: 1.10.0  (py35)

issue_title: data files end up in unexpected places, should replace with package_data
issue_body:
The setup.py file packages config files as `data_files`. Since we're using relative path for those, they end up in inappropriate places, for example the unexpected `~/.local/etc`.

A solution would be to use `package_data` instead in the setup.py file.

issue_title: _read_tle fails to handle bstar values that start with + character
issue_body:
_read_tle fails to handle bstar and mean_motion_sec_derivative values that start with + character.

Sometimes, these values starts with a '+'.

Example (from ISS):
1 25544U 98067A   15235.81765006 +.00009573 +00000-0 +14486-3 0  9999
2 25544 051.6452 106.3529 0001648 090.4174 004.9123 15.55401685958567

The + character is a valid character in this position, as indicated [by Celestrak](https://celestrak.com/columns/v04n03/:)
    
    "Columns with a '+' can have either a plus sign, a minus sign, or a space and columns with a '-' can have either a plus or minus sign (if the rest of the field is not blank)."

issue_title: Heartbeat max timeout
issue_body:
Let's make sure the heartbeat times out before 30 seconds. Otherwise uWSGI hara kiris the web head and we don't have a chance to send back the result of the heartbeat where it tells us which backend fails.

I suggest 15 seconds max by defaut + an option.

issue_title: changed metadata.yaml files require wayback restart to be available in templates

issue_title: ArgumentError:  Recommended column 'champion' conflicting with relationship property.
issue_body:
Hi all,
I've been using the automapping from SqlAlchemy and came across this error:

`ArgumentError: WARNING: when configuring property 'champion' on Mapper|Recommended|Recommended, column 'champion' conflicts with property '<RelationshipProperty at 0x7f639bc56c88; champion>'. To resolve this, map the column to the class under a different name in the 'properties' dictionary.  Or, to remove all awareness of the column entirely (including its availability as a foreign key), use the 'include_properties' or 'exclude_properties' mapper arguments to control specifically which table columns get mapped.`


Here's my code:
```
`from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.engine import create_engine

engine = create_engine("postgresql://", echo=False)

from sqlalchemy import MetaData, Table, Column, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.ext.automap import automap_base
# produce our own MetaData object
metadata = MetaData()

# we can reflect it ourselves from a database, using options
# such as 'only' to limit what tables we look at...
metadata.reflect(engine)

# we can then produce a set of mappings from this MetaData.
Base = automap_base(metadata=metadata)

# calling prepare() just sets up mapped classes and relationships.
Base.prepare()

# mapped classes are ready
player, match_detail, participant, participant_stats= Base.classes.MatchPlayer, Base.classes.MatchDetail, \
Base.classes.MatchParticipant, Base.classes.MatchParticipantStats

session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = session()

issue_title: Should featurize_dataframe have an exceptions_encountered() variable?
issue_body:
Let's say you run featurize_dataframe with ignore_errors=True. After finishing featurization, you might want to just see what errors were encountered. If the BaseFeaturizer had an attribute/function called exceptions_encountered() perhaps it could return all the exceptions encountered during the previous featurize_dataframe() call.

Implementation would be simple - just create the variable, reset it at the beginning of the featurize_dataframe, and use traceback module to add the string version of each Exception to a list.

Another way to do it would be to add another variable called ``return_errors`` to featurize_dataframe. If ``ignore_errors`` and ``return_errors`` are both true, then featurize_dataframe would change its return type to be a tuple of the new dataframe and a list of errors encountered. This would avoid having a state variable for the previous errors.

Maybe other ways to do it - but the point would be to be able to see a list of errors encountered during featurize_dataframe with ignore_errors = True.

issue_title: Cannot supply multiple files to pact-verifier
issue_body:
According to the [README](https://github.com/pact-foundation/pact-python#--pact-urls), to supply multiple local pact files you give the `--pact-urls` argument a comma separated list of files.

However, this will give you an error that the file cannot be found and that file is the combined paths, 
```
Error: The following Pact files could not be found:
/tmp/pacts/pact1.json,/tmp/pacts/pact2.json
```
when they should be separated by a [new line](https://github.com/pact-foundation/pact-python/blob/master/pact/verify.py#L69).

This is because for the click option `--pact-urls` the parameter `multiple=True` is used. However this is meant for providing multiple `--pact-urls` flags such as
```
pact-verifier --pact-urls=./provider/firstpact.json --pact-urls=./provider/secondpact.json
```
http://click.pocoo.org/5/options/#multiple-options

Either the documentation should be updated to list that you should provide an argument for each file, or remove `multiple=True` and just do a `.split(',')` when reading the pact-urls argument.

issue_title: Check failover logging
issue_body: Tests https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2525 ## Sanity Checking Start Pulp normally and perform a basic sync to sanity check Pulp. Start Pulp normally. Allow the system to idle for 40 seconds and observe the logs. Verify that no errors are reported while Pulp idles over 40 seconds. ## Resource Manager Failover Testing ### Testing normal concurrent operation Start Pulp normally, but keep the resource manager stopped. Then in one terminal on the box run one resource manager A with: sudo -u apache /bin/python /usr/bin/celery worker -A pulp.server.async.app -n resource_manager@boxA -Q resource_manager -c 1 --events --umask 18 --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/resource_managerA.pid --heartbeat-interval=5 On a second terminal run resource manager B with: sudo -u apache /bin/python /usr/bin/celery worker -A pulp.server.async.app -n resource_manager@boxB -Q resource_manager -c 1 --events --umask 18 --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/resource_managerB.pid --heartbeat-interval=5 Verify that you see the full Celery "banner" with stars in resource manager A. Verify that you see a log statement that resource manager A has acquired the lock. Verify that you do not see any output from resource manager B. Verify that you see a log statement that resource manager B is a hot spare. Verify that both resource_managers are reported via pulp-admin status ### Failover due to graceful shutdown Start both resource_manager started processes as described above. Ctrl+C from resource manager A Verify that resource manager B displays the celery banner with stars within 5 seconds of the Ctrl-C Verify that you see a log statement stating that failover has occurred, and resource manager B is the new primary resource manager Verify that only resource manager B is shown in pulp-admin status ### Failover due to killing Start both resource_manager started processes as described above. kill -9 the resource_manager that has acquired the lock Verify that resource manager B displays the celery banner with stars within 30 seconds of the kill Verify that you see a log statement stating that failover has occurred, and resource manager B is the new primary resource manager Verify that only resource manager B is shown in pulp-admin status ## Celerybeat Failover Testing This can't be done on 1 computer without changing Pulp code due to both celerybeats having the same name. You can do the same test on two machines with one celerybeat on one, and a second on another. I'm going to apply this diff to the code to allow me to do it on 1 machine in two separate terms. ```diff diff --git a/server/pulp/server/async/scheduler.py b/server/pulp/server/async/scheduler.py index d745406..b158386 100644 --- a/server/pulp/server/async/scheduler.py +++ b/server/pulp/server/async/scheduler.py @@ -28,8 +28,9 @@ import pulp.server.logs # noqa _logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) +import random # setting the celerybeat name -CELERYBEAT_NAME = constants.SCHEDULER_WORKER_NAME + "@" + platform.node() +CELERYBEAT_NAME = constants.SCHEDULER_WORKER_NAME + "@" + str(random.randint(1,10000)) class EventMonitor(threading.Thread): ``` ### Testing normal concurrency operation Start Pulp normally, but keep celerybeat stopped. Then in one terminal on the box run celerybeat A with: sudo -u apache /bin/python /usr/bin/celery beat --app=pulp.server.async.celery_instance.celery --scheduler=pulp.server.async.scheduler.Scheduler --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/celerybeatA.pid On a second terminal run celerybeat B with: sudo -u apache /bin/python /usr/bin/celery beat --app=pulp.server.async.celery_instance.celery --scheduler=pulp.server.async.scheduler.Scheduler --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/celerybeatB.pid Verify that pulp-admin status shows both scheduler@ entries Verify that the logs contain a statement like 'New lock acquired by scheduler@xxxxxxxx' Verify that a log statement exists identifying the extra celerybeat as a hot spare. ### Failover due to graceful shutdown Start both celerybeat processes as described above. Ctrl-C the celerybeat that has acquired the lock. Verify that within 5 seconds the logs emit a statement like New lock acquired by 'scheduler@yyyyyyy' Verify that you see a log statement stating that failover has occurred, and that the other celerybeat is the new primary celerybeat instance Verify that pulp-admin status shows only one scheduler@ entry Verify that no Errors are shown in the logs ### Failover due to killing Start both celerybeat processes as described above. kill -9 the pid of the celerybeat that has the lock. If you start celerybeatA first then it will get the lock. Verify that within 30 seconds the logs emit a statement like New lock acquired by 'scheduler@yyyyyyy Verify that you see a log statement stating that failover has occurred, and that the other celerybeat is the new primary celerybeat instance' Verify that an Error is emitted saying something like: `Worker 'scheduler@xxxx' has gone missing` Verify that pulp-admin status shows only one scheduler@ entry

import graphene

class Query(graphene.ObjectType):

schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query)

params = graphql_server.GraphQLParams('query { reverse(word: $name) }', {'name': "GraphQl User"}, None)

```

None

issue_title: Firewalld service state after watchmaker run with --no-reboot option

Prior to version 0.6.3 of watchmaker, use of the -n option to prevent immediate reboot after a watchmaker run, the firewalld service was left in a running state so that scripted firewall-cmd executions would succeed.  Since 0.6.3, at the end of a watchmaker run with the -n option, prior to reboot the firewalld service is left in a inactive/dead state.  This has required me to modify some install scripts, but didn't think this behavior was watchmaker's intent.

issue_title: Priority of visual_versions ConanMultiPackager's parameter vs CONAN_VISUAL_VERSIONS
issue_body:
_Disclaimer:_ I am a newbie using Conan and GitHub, so probably what I am proposing here is completely wrong, or the style is not correct... Sorry for that.

I am creating a package for a library that, under Windows, is only compilable using Visual Studio 2013 (12) and 2015 (14). I am trying to show this restriction in the build.py file, so looks like this:

```
from conan.packager import ConanMultiPackager
if __name__ == "__main__":
    builder = ConanMultiPackager(username="demo")
    builder.add_common_builds(visual_versions = ["12", "14"])
    builder.run()
```

In my machine, I only have VS 2013 installed so, in order to build the packages, I tried this:

```
set CONAN_VISUAL_VERSIONS="12" 
python build.py
```

However, the script tried to build the package using both VS 2013 and 2015 which caused a build error. Checking the code of [packager.py](https://github.com/conan-io/conan-package-tools/blob/0233c70780398db777780f7b0e39d7b1e125707d/conan/packager.py) I found:

```
if visual_versions is not None:
    self.visual_versions = visual_versions
else:
    env_visual_versions = list(filter(None, os.getenv("CONAN_VISUAL_VERSIONS", "").split(",")))
    self.visual_versions = env_visual_versions or self.default_visual_versions
```

That is, the ConanMultiPackager's parameter has priority over the environment variable. However, in my opinion, the environment variable should have a higher priority, as it would allow to cover cases like this (a machine with only a VS environment installed) without having to modify the script.

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: [ip6] memory leak when passing frame from NCP to Host
issue_body:
Hello,

**Background:**
Recently I have found a serious issue while performing OpenThread NCP stress tests.

I have 24x NCP devices (nRF52840) in my setup that flood network (approx. for ~8h) with Echo Request packets on FF03::1 with different sizes. I control devices using pyspinel wpan API.

The result i found is that after test i see a flood of _MLE Parent Request_ messages. The end result is that all devices become Thread Leaders.

I saw also some posts from other users that discovered this problem with a 2-3 devices in the network.

**Investigation:**
I simply rebuilt firmware without optimization and put breakpoint in the `MleRouter::HandleParentRequest`, then i found that `MleRouter::SendParentResponse` fails because there is no free buffers (even couple of hours after performing stress tests).

To find a memory leak i have added profiling method to `Message` class e.g:

```
VerifyOrExit((message = NewMleMessage()) != NULL, error = OT_ERROR_NO_BUFS);

#if OPENTHREAD_CONFIG_MESSAGE_PROFILING
    message->SetConsumerFuncName(__PRETTY_FUNCTION__);
#endif
```

Also i set `ConsumerFuncName` to NULL in `MessagePool::Free` method.

_Side Note:  I don't feel this profiling method is the greatest, though i haven't find better way. Anyway, do you think that this kind of profiling has potential to be a part of OpenThread?_

After problem occurred again i printed logs from the GDB, and voilà, a lot of buffers pointed to `Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback`:

```
      mHead = {
            mInfo = {
              mNext = {0x2000486c <ot::sInstanceRaw+15964>, 0x2000486c <ot::sInstanceRaw+15964>},
              mPrev = {0x200030ec <ot::sInstanceRaw+9948>, 0x200030ec <ot::sInstanceRaw+9948>},
              mMessagePool = 0x20002a68 <ot::sInstanceRaw+8280>,
              mQueue = {
                mMessage = 0x200000f8 <ot::Ncp::sNcpRaw+32>,
                mPriority = 0x200000f8 <ot::Ncp::sNcpRaw+32>
              },
              mReserved = 0,
              mLength = 84,
              mOffset = 40,
              [...]
              mTxSuccess = false,
              mConsumerFuncName = 0x5a9e0 <ot::Ip6::Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback(ot::Message const&, ot::Ip6::MessageInfo const&, unsigned char, bool)::__PRETTY_FUNCTION__> "otError ot::Ip6::Ip6::ProcessReceiveCallback(const ot::Message&, const ot::Ip6::MessageInfo&, uint8_t, bool)"
            },
           [...]
```

I am not sure yet where the problem is, i have two candidates and i am going to check them, however i would appreciate if you @jwhui can look on this as well.

issue_title: Path cost calculation errors
issue_body:
During my work on #2775 I've been testing how path cost is being calculated, and I've noticed some oddities. Maybe it is me not understanding the fine details of the Thread spec, but more likely this is some bug. Here it goes:

First, I've been testing 'long line' kind of topology: devices are in line, each device can see only 1 neighbour each side, end ones can see only 1 neighbour. Pretty much identical to #2782. Each link was having RSSI set to `-90`, which should correspond to route cost of `4` on each hop.

I have WIP implementation of 'long routes' mode, where I can form a line of >4 routers. For starting, I've been playing with 8 of them. When I collected all their path costs taken from `router table` output, the table was mostly OK, but with 2 strange behaviours:

from \ to | dc00 | 8c00 | bc00 | 5c00 | 3c00 | 7800 | b000 | 1000
--------- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ----
dc00 | 0 | *0* | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16 | 20 | 24
8c00 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16 | 20
bc00 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16
5c00 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12
3c00 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8
7800 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4
b000 | 20 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8
1000 | 24 | 20 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | *0* | 0

How to read a table: row header is source rloc16, column header is destination rloc16. IDs are arranged in topology order, so rloc `dc00` was at one end of the line, while `1000` was the other end. So, `route table` output for `dc00` shown that it thinks that the cost to `8c00` is `0`, whereas `8c00` thinks that its cost to `dc00` is `8`.

As you see following 2 problems are striking:
- typically each node think that the cost to closest neighbour is `8`, the cost to one after that is `4`, and then all other costs are too small by `4`. I.e. I would expect cost from `dc00` to `1000` to be `28` (7 hops) not `24`.
- cost from end nodes to their closest neighbours is `0` for some reason. It is probably related to the 'next hop' value from `router table` being `63`.

Is that how it is supposed to work? I've expected pretty much `0 - 4 - 8 - ... - 24 - 28` kind of output.

So, I suspected, that my PR had broken something. I did a clean checkout of current master, and re-tried with 4 nodes. Here is the table:

to \ from | dc00 | 8c00 | bc00 | 5c00
--------- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ----
dc00 | 0 | *0* | 4 | 8
8c00 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4
bc00 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8
5c00 | 8 | 4 | *0* | 0

Which is consistent with the larger table.

Interestingly, lack of `3c00` node changed `5c00` -> `bc00` cost from `8` to `0`.

Also, if the max cost is 16, I should be able to add 1 more node and see 12 in the table, right?

After a bit closer digging, I've also noticed that 'next hop' value get weirdly reported. On each node I did `router 0x5c00`, and node `bc00` said that the next hop is `8c00`, whereas it should be directly `5c00`. 

Another oddity is that the LQ In / LQ Out values in table, sometimes shown `1` instead of `0`. But, there was no `2` or `6` in the route cost table...

Also, I waited 5 real-time minutes (nodes were run with `20` time speedup factor) before issuing `router table` command, to make sure that the routing costs stabilized properly.

Here is the cli dump of all 4 nodes:

```

issue_title: Artman debug cmd string should set --local
issue_body:
When constructing debug_cmd here (https://github.com/googleapis/artman/blob/c9c95b578551e2aa19584a5215fa7408d48118af/artman/cli/main.py#L468) we should set the `--local` flag if it is not already set. Otherwise, the artman command running in the Docker image will also try to use Docker, resulting in this error:

`artman> Docker not found on path or is not installed. Refert to https://docs.docker.com/engine/installation about how to install Docker on your local machine.`

cc @ethanbao

issue_title: Remote execution fails when local_repo is too big
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

When the directory specified by the local_repo dir is too big, remote execution fails with the confusing message "taskflow.exceptions.StorageFailure: Storage backend internal error". This is a frequent error case if the directory pointed to is actually a git repo (i.e., also contains associated git metadata).

### Expected behavior

Artman should validate that the size of local_repo is < 1 MB.

### Action

Accumulate the size of local_repo here: https://github.com/googleapis/artman/blob/e83b08313c7fff2cb1f5ca6f716220050d1d4fd9/execute_pipeline.py#L221
and throw a meaningful exception if it is too big.

Define init data for [`BatchAnnotateImages` method](https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/cloud/vision/v1/vision_gapic.yaml#L36) in the Cloud Vision API.

The `BatchAnnotateImages` method takes a rich set of arguments to compensate for it being the only method in the Cloud Vision API, so good samples are really useful. issue_title: spin Aspen out into a new org
issue_body:
Aspen is currently housed under Gratipay. At https://github.com/gratipay/aspen.py/issues/526#issuecomment-174446901, @Changaco suggests spinning it out into its own entity:

> I had a thought last night: if aspen becomes a framework that can integrate into others, then it should be easy to integrate it with a nano-framework based on `algorithm.py`, which for lack of a better name (https://github.com/gratipay/algorithm.py/issues/3) could be called `aspen-standalone`, and eventually `aspen-legacy` if we all migrate away from it.
>
I think we should use aspen as an umbrella name, split it into separate libraries (`aspen-{fs-routing,simplates}` for the core and `aspen-{django,flask,standalone}` for the shells), and move all of those into separate repositories under a new aspen organization. The work in #527 wouldn't be lost, it would be the base for everything except the `standalone` shell.

issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):

Ubuntu Trusty 14.04.5 LTS

## My web server is (include version):

nginx (but not relevant, I've been using the standalone here.

## How did you install Certbot:

using `pip3 install letsencrypt`.

## What command did you run and what output did it produce?

```
% certbot certonly --standalone -d example.org -d […]
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          -------------------------------------------------------------------------------                                                                                                                          You have an existing certificate that contains a portion of the domains you                                                                                                                              requested (ref: /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/example.org.conf)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        It contains these names: example.org […]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   You requested these names for the new certificate: example.org, […].                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Do you want to expand and replace this existing certificate with the new                                                                                                                                 certificate?                                                                                                                                                                                             -------------------------------------------------------------------------------                                                                                                                          (E)xpand/(C)ancel: e                                                                                                                                                                                     Renewing an existing certificate                                                                                                                                                                         Performing the following challenges:                                                                                                                                                                     tls-sni-01 challenge for example.org
[…]
Waiting for verification...                                                                                                                                                                              Cleaning up challenges                                                                                                                                                                                   Generating key (2048 bits): /etc/letsencrypt/keys/0007_key-certbot.pem                                                                                                                                   Creating CSR: /etc/letsencrypt/csr/0007_csr-certbot.pem                                                                                                                                                  An unexpected error occurred:                                                                                                                                                                            TypeError: must be str, not bytes                                                                                                                                                                        Please see the logfiles in /var/log/letsencrypt for more details.                                
```

## Can you provide a Certbot error log showing the issue?
###### It is stored by default in `/var/log/letsencrypt` - feel free to redact domain names, e-mail and IP addresses as you see fit

```
2017-02-26 23:55:31,201:DEBUG:certbot.storage:Writing new private key to /etc/letsencrypt/archive/example.org/privkey9.pem.
2017-02-26 23:55:31,202:DEBUG:certbot.main:Exiting abnormally:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/certbot", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "…/certbot/main.py", line 882, in main
    return config.func(config, plugins)
  File "…/certbot/main.py", line 659, in obtain_cert
    action, _ = _auth_from_available(le_client, config, domains, certname, lineage)
  File "…/certbot/main.py", line 104, in _auth_from_available
    renewal.renew_cert(config, domains, le_client, lineage)
  File "…/certbot/renewal.py", line 306, in renew_cert
    lineage.save_successor(prior_version, new_cert, new_key.pem, new_chain, config)
  File "…/certbot/storage.py", line 1062, in save_successor
    f.write(new_privkey)
TypeError: must be str, not bytes
```

## Workaround to make it work

in the /usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/certbot/storage.py file I added the following function at module scope:

```
ensure_str = lambda s: s if isinstance(s, str) else s.decode('utf8')
```

and used it at the end to make sure str were being written to the files:

```
1062         else:
1063             with open(target["privkey"], "w") as f:
1064                 logger.debug("Writing new private key to %s.", target["privkey"])
1065                 f.write(ensure_str(new_privkey))
1066                                                                                                                                                                                                     1067         # Save everything else
1068         with open(target["cert"], "w") as f:
1069             logger.debug("Writing certificate to %s.", target["cert"])
1070             f.write(ensure_str(new_cert))
1071         with open(target["chain"], "w") as f:
1072             logger.debug("Writing chain to %s.", target["chain"])
1073             f.write(ensure_str(new_chain))
1074         with open(target["fullchain"], "w") as f:
1075             logger.debug("Writing full chain to %s.", target["fullchain"])
1076             f.write(ensure_str(new_cert) + ensure_str(new_chain))
```

That solution is pretty dirty, but it works.

issue_title: Remove timeouts from the onchain calls of the REST API
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

As discussed many times in the past and more recently [here](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/1937#issuecomment-408241015), having timeouts for the blockchain state is not really helping because once the timeout exception is thrown we are left in a state where there is an on-chain transaction in flight and the logic of the call is not continuing.

But the transaction will eventually be mined and we will have to deal it through the processing of the events it will generate.

Timeouts can be instead used by the HTTP client (e.g. WebUI, curl, httpie) but there is no point in having them inside the Rest API itself.

## Task

Remove alll timeouts from the API calls that are waiting for on-chain transactions to get mined. So essentially:

- [Token Network Register](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e3bf771c9e091c22576581f7cdd021a6f3b711b4/raiden/api/python.py#L118)
- [Channel Open](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e3bf771c9e091c22576581f7cdd021a6f3b711b4/raiden/api/python.py#L255)
- [Set Channel Deposit](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e3bf771c9e091c22576581f7cdd021a6f3b711b4/raiden/api/python.py#L373)
- [Channel Close](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e3bf771c9e091c22576581f7cdd021a6f3b711b4/raiden/api/python.py#L472)

issue_title: Inconsistency in dim ordering between ImageClassifierData.from_arrays and PyTorch models
issue_body:
The issue is illustrated here: https://gist.github.com/raspstephan/df9bb81b954851e723938d74427c3ca5

In ImageClassifierData.from_arrays the attribute .sz is derived from x.shape[1], while PyTorch models need the channel dimension in the 1st axis. This prohibits the use of from_arrays with pretrained models as shown in the minimal nb.

If I am not missing something, I would suggest using [nsamples, channels, x, y] and therefore sz = x.shape[2] in from_arrays and adding documentation.

I would be happy to prepare a pull request, but wanted to make sure first I am not mistaken.

issue_title: TPOTBase.score doesn't impute values like TPOTBase.predict does
issue_body:
> TPOT's imputation step isn't stored in the internal pipelines, but it should apply imputation when you call any functions like fit, predict, score, etc.

Here's the code inside `TPOTBase(BaseEstimator).predict`

```
features = features.astype(np.float64)

if np.any(np.isnan(features)):
    self._imputed = True
    features = self._impute_values(features)
else:
    self._imputed = False
return self.fitted_pipeline_.predict(features)
```

So yeah, it will use the `self._fitted_imputer` when predicting because of the `self._impute_values` call.

But now here's the code inside `TPOTBase(BaseEstimator).score`:

```
score = SCORERS[self.scoring_function](
            self.fitted_pipeline_,
            testing_features.astype(np.float64),
            testing_target.astype(np.float64)
)
```

It simply forwards the `testing_features` without using the `self._fitted_imputer`, so unlike I expected, it won't do imputation automatically when using the `score` method.

  agent = Agent(name, batchsize, config)

  
  collected_rollouts = [agent.rollout() for _ in range(20)]
  collected_rollouts = ray.get(collected_rollouts)

  end_time = time.time()
  duration = end_time - start_time

  expected_duration = np.ceil(20 / psutil.cpu_count()) * 0.5
  assert duration < expected_duration, ("Rollouts took {} seconds. This is "
                                        "too slow.".format(duration))

  print("Success! The example took {} seconds.".format(duration))
```

issue_title: Add support to open schemas
issue_body:
Right now the OpenAPI 2.0 spec is dubious on how to handle this, but several services like `codegen` allow defining open schemas by setting `additionalProperties: {}`. I guess it should be added, specially if the colander mapping is set to type `colander.Mapping(unknown='preserve')`.

Original discussion on swagger-codegen: https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-codegen/issues/1318#issuecomment-146302879

issue_title: Allow certain field labels to show even when form_show_labels=False
issue_body:
There are cases where you might want all field labels to be hidden (for example if you're going to put the label in the text field placeholder attribute instead), but for certain fields with no placeholder attribute like checkboxes, it would be useful to allow setting a field label that would appear.

Could an option of 'show_label' be added to Field so that it overrides the form_show_labels value set on the form. So if form_show_labels is False, only those fields with show_label=True would show labels. And if form_show_labels is True, only fields with show_label=False would hide the label.

issue_title: 100% cpu usage
issue_body:
There seems to be an issue in the python 0.8.0 version which occasionally causes websockify to get stuck at 100% cpu usage. The problem disappears when reverting to 0.7.0. This has happened on both a Centos 6 server, and a Ubuntu server. Normally cpu usage is < 2% per websockify process.

Here is the stack trace (probably not useful):

#0  0x0000712e54be059d in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#1  0x0000712e54be5647 in PyEval_EvalCodeEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#2  0x0000712e54be3a94 in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#3  0x0000712e54be5647 in PyEval_EvalCodeEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#4  0x0000712e54be3a94 in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#5  0x0000712e54be4b7f in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#6  0x0000712e54be4b7f in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#7  0x0000712e54be4b7f in PyEval_EvalFrameEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#8  0x0000712e54be5647 in PyEval_EvalCodeEx ()
   from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#9  0x0000712e54b78ca0 in ?? () from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#10 0x0000712e54b51c63 in PyObject_Call () from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#11 0x0000712e54b646af in ?? () from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0
#12 0x0000712e54b51c63 in PyObject_Call () from /usr/lib64/libpython2.6.so.1.0

The process doesn't seem to be stuck...it exits normally when the user disconnects.

Here is the process listing for a few websockify processes:

root     29597 24.0  0.0 196368  9216 ?        R    09:07  38:01 python /home/packages/websockify-master/websockify.py
root     29599  0.3  0.0 196500  9404 ?        S    09:07   0:35 python /home/packages/websockify-master/websockify.py

Both processes here have been running 2.5hrs. One has used 38 minutes of cpu time during that interval, and the other just 35 seconds. This is not to do with usage...even with our highest packet throughput these processes typically only use 2% of cpu, even with 0.8.0. I think there is some factor that is occasionally causing 0.8.0 to sometimes use all the cpu time.

issue_body:
Currently, we have run into instances where netCDF files have a nodata/_FillValue value of NaN (strange, but it happened). The current code in odc does not correctly search for the location of nodata  in the case of a NaN value (https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/develop/datacube/storage/storage.py#L195, and https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/develop/datacube/storage/storage.py#L162). It is a simple fix to use `numpy.isnan` to support this. 

We have developed a fix for this, would you be interested in a PR?

issue_title: Discussion of TensorLayer 2.0
Discrete State Space Model Object Support
issue_body:
I'd like to request object support for state space models, especially discrete state space. These are supported in APM MATLAB and APM Python. 
https://github.com/APMonitor/apm_python/blob/master/example_lti_regression/
https://github.com/APMonitor/apm_matlab/tree/master/example_lti

Here is an example model: http://apmonitor.com/wiki/index.php/Apps/LinearStateSpace

The discrete state space model should be able to link with other models. If a discrete state space model is used, a uniform time discretization should be enforced or a warning issued if there are non-uniform time intervals.

There is also the apm_id function in APM MATLAB and APM Python that identifies Linear Time-Invariant (LTI) models from data and writes the APM model. Identification can be a separate / future effort. This request is to add support for state space models.
